perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall apply only to PCQ and ABW.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'all of their subsidiaries and related companies,' the scope of the amendment is drastically narrowed. This creates an in-text contradiction because other parts of the original Co-Branding Agreement likely extend obligations to subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This amendment to Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall apply only to PCQ and ABW. [***] Source: PCQUOTE COM INC, S-1/A, 7/21/1999 AGREED TO BY: /s/ Steven Malin /s/ Howard Meltzer - -------------------------- --------------------------------- A.B. Watley, Inc. PC Quote, Inc. Mr. Steven Malin Mr. Howard Meltzer Director President Date: 12/5/96 Date: 12/12/96 -------------- ---------------- Source: PCQUOTE COM INC, S-1/A, 7/21/1999
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of the amendment to only PCQ and ABW, omitting 'all of their subsidiaries and related companies.' The contradicted text, which is in fact the same clause in the document, initially states the amendment applies only to PCQ and ABW. However, the explanation states that other parts of the original Co-Branding Agreement likely extend obligations to subsidiaries and related companies. Therefore, by omitting this language elsewhere in the amendment's description, a contradiction has been introduced. The contradiction exists due to the omission creating conflicting interpretations of the amendment's scope, leading to uncertainty and potential enforceability issues.
